vitals.surface: mostly paved roads, minor off-road options
vitals.participation_trend: growing interest in ultra events by Lost Dot

course_description.overview: Self-supported ultra race from Girona (Mediterranean) across the Pyrenees to Orio (Atlantic), then back via iconic cols to Girona, featuring mandatory parcours and control points with ~1800km of relentless climbing through Catalonia, Basque country, and high Pyrenees.

key_segments.name: Parcours A: Els Angels to Coll d’Arques
key_segments.description: Tarmac with 17m gain, leading to cross-country choices through forested slopes.

key_segments.name: Parcours B: Coll de Jou to Coll de Boixols
key_segments.description: Balcony roads with sheer rock faces and endless views in unexplored areas.

key_segments.name: CP1: Pessonada (Boumort Reserve)
key_segments.description: Unmanned analog checkpoint; tear page from book, tribute to Barkley Marathons.

key_segments.name: Parcours C: Plan to Canon de Anisclo (CP2)
key_segments.description: Skims Pyrenees edge with high peaks and Ordesa cliffs.

key_segments.name: RAID Pyreneen (Parcours E/F)
key_segments.description: Historic cols including Marie-Blanque, Aubisque, Soulor, Tourmalet, Aspin, Peyresourde; finishes at Cim de Coma Morera (2200m).

key_segments.name: Finish: Panta de Susqueda to Girona
key_segments.description: Secondary roads and Via Verde along Ter river.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Considerable climbing for grimpeurs, demanding terrain rewarding adventure, with route choices, remote areas, and high-altitude cols up to 2200m.

logistics.getting_there: Start/finish in Girona, Spain; accessible by flights to Girona-Costa Brava Airport or Barcelona; riders manage own travel to start.
logistics.accommodation: Self-supported; riders source own bivouac or hotels en route.
logistics.registration_details: Applications open 23rd January - 15th February 2026; Registration: 24th September 2026 10:00-16:00 CEST; Cost: £415 per rider + £130 refundable tracker deposit; solo or pairs.
logistics.bike_logistics: Riders responsible for bike transport, maintenance, and navigation; tracker required.
logistics.local_considerations: Pyrenees weather variable; prepare for remote areas, high altitude, and border crossings between France/Spain.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic self-supported Pyrenees double-crossing for grimpeurs seeking ultimate adventure.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced ultra-distance cyclists, solo or pairs, who thrive on climbing, navigation choices, and remoteness.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for those chasing Mike Hall's vision of demanding, rewarding terrain in stunning scenery.
pro_tips: Master route choices at parcours transitions for time savings.
Pack for variable mountain weather and high altitude.
Train extensively on long climbs; focus on grimpeur fitness.
Validate CPs analog-style; carry book page tools.
